content.full_text:
April 18, 1991

Fair Political Practices Commission
Legal Division
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95814

Re: Request for Advice Letter.

Gentlemen:

I am the City Attorney for the City of Huntington Park. Please provide me with an advice letter regarding the facts and questions set forth below.

Current City Council Members William P. Cunningham, Thomas E. Jackson and Jack W. Parks have requested that the City provide a defense for them in a criminal matter, pursuant to Government Code, section 995.8. All three persons are involved in the same law enforcement investigation. The questions presented are:

(1) Does each councilman have a conflict of interest in voting on whether the City should provide him with a defense?

(2) Does each councilman have a conflict of interest in voting on whether the City should provide the other councilmen with a defense?

(3) If each councilman has a conflict of interest as to voting on whether to provide a defense for himself and for the others, can any of the three councilmen vote on any of these issues, based on the lack of a quorum (there will be only two remaining, completely uninvolved councilmen)?

Please contact me if you need more information. Thank you for your assistance.

Very truly yours,

Steven N. Skolnik
